question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the endpoint GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/tokens with thousands of concurrent requests, each triggering getAccountTokenAllowances() and holding a database connection via super.getRows(), exhausting the connection pool in pool() and causing all subsequent mirror node queries—including those needed for transaction confirmation—to queue indefinitely, resulting in total service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with both lower and upper bound spender.id filters and a token.id filter, causing getQuery() to produce a three-branch union all query where each branch independently executes select * from token_allowance with its own ORDER BY and LIMIT clause, tripling the database load per single HTTP request and enabling a small number of concurrent attackers to saturate the database server, preventing transaction-related queries from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause getSubQuery() to be called three times per getQuery() invocation—once each for lower, inner, and upper filter arrays—each call appending to the shared mutable params array and issuing a separate parameterized SQL query with ORDER BY spender, token_id and LIMIT $2, causing the database to perform three sequential sort operations per request and enabling CPU exhaustion on the database server under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the params array is initialized as [ownerAccountId, limit] and then mutated by reference across multiple getSubQuery() calls, causing parameter index drift where a filter value pushed in the first subquery occupies position $3 while the second subquery's filter occupies $4, and if any upstream validation gap allows an extra filter to be injected, the parameter positions shift causing a malformed query that crashes the database session and propagates as an unhandled rejection crashing the Node.js process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high-limit request (e.g., limit=25 with three active subqueries) causing getAccountTokenAllowances() to receive up to 75 rows from the union all before the outer limit is applied, then rows.map((ta) => new TokenAllowance(ta)) allocates 75 JavaScript objects per request, and under sustained concurrent load this causes Node.js heap exhaustion, triggering an out-of-memory crash that shuts down the REST API service entirely?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static accountTokenAllowanceQuery = 'select * from token_allowance' which selects all columns including amount, amount_granted, owner, spender, token_id, payer_account_id, and timestamp_range, causing each subquery to transfer the full row width across the database connection, and under high concurrency this saturates the database-to-application network bandwidth, starving legitimate transaction-processing queries of network capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests targeting accounts with a very large number of token allowances, causing the union all query built in getQuery() to perform full or near-full scans of the token_allowance table for each of the three subqueries, consuming excessive I/O and CPU on the database server and causing query timeouts that cascade into connection pool exhaustion and service shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where filter.operator is a value that, if not strictly validated upstream, causes getSubQuery() to embed an unexpected operator string directly into the SQL condition string '${column}${filter.operator}$${params.length}', producing a syntactically invalid SQL statement that causes the database driver to throw an unhandled exception, crashing the Node.js worker process and taking the REST API offline?
